body.title: Enforce logging rule: every state change + every interface crossing
body.result: logging rule documented in CLAUDE.md; AR-009 (audit_check_interface_logging.py) already committed; granny already retrofitted; workspace_run_bash violation handed off to T-workspace-file-tools
body.description:
Current logging in devices is ad-hoc — some log state changes, most don't log interface crossings. The canonical rule is: log every major state change AND every interface crossing. Interface crossings are: method calls that cross device/shim boundaries, MCP tool dispatch, channel post/read, DB write/read, subprocess spawn. State changes are: ticket status transitions, device lifecycle (start/stop/restart/halt), routing decisions, auth/trust events.

This rule should be: (1) written into CLAUDE.md and TheoryOfOperation §logging, (2) added as a forever audit check via audit_add.py that scans device classes for interface-crossing methods without a log call, (3) retrofitted into GrannyWeatherwaxDevice as the first example.

GrannyWeatherwaxDevice already logs routing decisions but does NOT log: _dispatch_to_cc subprocess spawn result (success/failure/pid), _post_to_channel call, register_worker calls, strengthen/weaken_edge calls.

**Affected files:** CLAUDE.md (add logging rule), lab/claudecode/audit_add.py (register check), devices/granny/device.py (retrofit as first example)
**Design rules:** observability > new capabilities (per project memory); logs must be structured enough to be greppable by device and event type
**Scope boundary:** IN — document the rule, register the audit check, retrofit granny; OUT — retrofitting all other devices (follow-on)
**Completion criteria:** CLAUDE.md has the logging rule stated; audit_runner flags device methods that cross interfaces without logging; granny/device.py logs every dispatch, channel post, subprocess spawn, and worker registration
